steps.title: Identify what the goal demands
steps.content: The student wants to explain an advantage of the Oak Tree House dwelling site. An effective answer must name a specific benefit that Oak Tree House offered its inhabitants. The relevant note: "The overhanging rock ledges offered protection from heavy rain and snow." Any correct choice must state that advantage explicitly, not merely assert that one exists.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — The location of Oak Tree House, an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site in southwestern Colorado, provided an advantage to its inhabitants.: Claims an advantage exists but never names it; fails to explain anything. ✗
  • Choice B — Located in southwestern Colorado, Oak Tree House is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site that was inhabited from approximately 1180–1210 CE.: Gives location and dates only; no advantage mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — The relatively flat terrain on which Oak Tree House was built allowed for the construction of large terraced buildings.: Describes Yucca House's advantage (flat terrain + terraced buildings), not Oak Tree House's. ✗
  • Choice D — Since it was built under a rock overhang, Oak Tree House was naturally protected from heavy rain and snow.: Identifies the site feature (rock overhang) and states its benefit (protection from rain and snow) — a complete explanation of the advantage. ✓
D is correct.
